source: OCJ Brampton Family Practice Direction (November 29, 2021, as amended March 22, 2022)
heading: Page Limits for Case Conference and Settlement Conference Briefs
text: At the OCJ Brampton, case conference and settlement conference briefs are limited to 10 pages in total including any attachments, except for professional reports, calculations, and any offers to settle. If counsel need to file expanded briefs, leave must be obtained from the case management judge. Orders from other courts that are relevant should be included and are not counted in the page limits. There is no need to include orders or endorsements from matters in this court as the justice will have access to the file. Briefs that exceed these page limits will not be accepted by the court for filing and may result in a costs order for a wasted court attendance.

source: OCJ Brampton Family Practice Direction (November 29, 2021, as amended March 22, 2022)
heading: Filing Timelines for Conference Briefs and Confirmation Forms (17F)
text: Briefs should be filed within the timelines set out in the Family Law Rules. Consents to late filing of briefs should not be used as this practice does not permit court staff sufficient time to process the briefs and forward them to the case management judge. Form 17F confirmation forms must be filed by email for every conference at least three days in advance.

source: OCJ Brampton Family Practice Direction (November 29, 2021, as amended March 22, 2022)
heading: Fast Track to Settlement Conference
text: Under Rule 17(7.1) of the Family Law Rules, matters that have proceeded through a dispute resolution process can be fast-tracked to a settlement conference. Parties can obtain a settlement conference date at the First Appearance if all pleadings are completed and they file a certificate from a recognized dispute resolution service. The Peel Family Mediation Service will have approved certificates. The dispute resolution process must have actually occurred for a certificate to be issued — this does not include matters where the parties are screened out of mediation or where a party fails to attend for a scheduled mediation appointment. Parties are strongly encouraged to consider mediation where appropriate. Peel Family Mediation Services contact information: Phone: 905-453-7795, Fax: 905-453-7796, Email: info@peelfamilymediation.org, Website: www.peelfamilymediation.org.

source: OCJ Brampton Family Practice Direction (November 29, 2021, as amended March 22, 2022)
heading: Page Limits for Motion Affidavits
text: At the OCJ Brampton, the page limit for affidavits in support of a motion is 10 pages, plus 6 pages of exhibits. The page limit for affidavits in support of a Form 14B motion is 4 pages, plus 4 pages of exhibits. Affidavits that exceed these page limits will not be accepted by the court for filing and may result in a costs order for a wasted court attendance. There is no need to include orders or endorsements from matters in this court as the justice will have access to the file. If a party believes that a longer affidavit is necessary, leave must be obtained by Form 14B Motion or from the case management judge.

source: OCJ Brampton Family Practice Direction (November 29, 2021, as amended March 22, 2022)
heading: Filing Timelines for Motion Materials and Confirmation Forms (14C)
text: Motion material should be filed within the timelines set out in the Family Law Rules. Consents to late filing of motion material should not be used, as this practice does not permit court staff sufficient time to process the material and forward it to the case management judge. Any references to case law or statutes can be made by hyperlink to CanLII. Form 14C confirmation forms must be filed by email for every motion at least three days in advance.

source: OCJ Brampton Family Practice Direction (November 29, 2021, as amended March 22, 2022)
heading: Modes of Appearance — OCJ Brampton Family
text: Paragraphs 1-7 of the November 29, 2021 Practice Direction regarding modes of appearance were revoked and replaced by the Direction of the Chief Justice of the OCJ dated March 18, 2022 which sets out modes of appearance in Family Court after April 4, 2022. That document is posted on the public website of the Ontario Court of Justice. In the transition period, counsel and parties may have matters scheduled for virtual hearings in some courts and in person attendances in others. Counsel or parties must advise the court of any potential conflicts and the court will do whatever can be done to ensure that matters in other courts or other court buildings are not double booked. The goal is to ensure that counsel and parties will be able to call into virtual hearings from their home or office and still be able to attend at any required in person matters.

heading: First Appearances — Service and Filing Requirements at OCJ Brampton
text: At OCJ Brampton, parties must file affidavits of personal service of originating materials (Applications or Motions to Change). Parties must not file affidavits of email service of originating documents without providing an order authorizing email service. If the file contains the Applicant's or moving party's pleadings and proof of proper service, the First Appearance Clerk (FAC) can schedule the matter into an initial case conference (ICC) slot. If proper proof of service is not in the file: (a) if the Respondent does not call into the conference, the matter will be referred to the back-up judge for a possible dismissal; (b) if the Respondent calls in and verbally acknowledges service, the matter will be adjourned to a new FA date at least two weeks away for the Applicant to file the affidavit of proper service or for the Respondent to file responding pleadings; (c) if the Respondent has not filed pleadings but does call in, the FAC may permit same-day email filing of the affidavit of service, or permit counsel for the Respondent to email a letter confirming they will go on the record, or refer to the back-up judge. Counsel should not count on these options.

heading: Validating Email Service Without Prior Court Order — OCJ Brampton
text: If counsel have served originating documents by email without a prior court order authorizing email service, they may file a 14B motion form and affidavit to validate service. The affidavit in support must include evidence that: (1) personal service could not be achieved; and (2) the email address the documents were sent to was recently used by the responding party. The motions judge will consider all of the evidence in determining whether service should be validated.

source: Ontario Court of Justice — Case Center Guide
heading: Case Center — What Cases and Events Use It
text: Case Center is a cloud-based document sharing and e-hearing platform developed by Thomson Reuters, currently free to use. Counsel and parties receive an email invitation from Thomson Reuters if their case qualifies. Cases currently using Case Center include: domestic family matters (Children's Law Reform Act and Family Law Act cases); Family Responsibility Office support enforcement cases in Toronto region (filed October 14, 2025 onward). Events supported: case conferences, settlement conferences, combined conferences, trial management conferences, motions with notice, and first appearance court (if the responding party filed materials at least 5 business days prior). Events NOT using Case Center: first appearances without responding materials filed 5 days ahead, uncontested trials, unopposed final orders, Interjurisdictional Support Orders Act hearings, motions filed within 4 days, ex-parte motions, procedural/unopposed motions, consent motions (Forms 15C/15D), international child abduction claims, CYFSA hearings, and support enforcement hearings outside Toronto.

source: Ontario Court of Justice — Case Center Guide
section: Case Center — Upload Timeline and Requirements
heading: Case Center — Document Upload Deadline and Filing
text: Documents filed outside the Ontario Courts Public Portal must be uploaded to Case Center at least 5 days in advance of the hearing, or at the same time as any filing deadlines that are less than 5 days. Case Center does NOT replace the filing of your documents with the court office — documents must still be filed per the 'Scheduling of Family Matters at the Ontario Court of Justice' notice. Parties who file through the Ontario Courts Public Portal do not need to upload directly to Case Center — court staff automatically post those documents. Non-portal filers must upload their filed documents themselves. Provide email addresses on all filed and uploaded documents. If documents are not named per the naming protocol, the judge may decide to review only those documents that comply and may reschedule the hearing.

source: Ontario Court of Justice — Case Center Guide
heading: Case Center — How to Name Documents
text: Documents uploaded to Case Center must follow this naming format: (1) Document type with form number; (2) Party type (Applicant, Respondent, Other); (3) Party name with initials if needed; (4) Date in DD-MMM-YYYY format. Example: 'Application Form 8 – Applicant – P. Smith – 12-JAN-2021'. Abbreviations allowed: APP, RESP, O, CAS, FRO, OCL. Add numbers at the start for ordering, e.g. '1. Case Conference Brief 17A – Respondent – Smith – 01-JAN-2021'. Document limitations: maximum 500 pages per file (split larger documents into volumes); page numbers should be placed at top-left or centre, not top-right (Case Center auto-numbers there); no password protection; one document per file (except affidavits with exhibits and books of authorities, which should be uploaded as a single PDF with hyperlinks).

source: Ontario Court of Justice — Case Center Guide
section: Case Center — What to Upload in Each Section
heading: Case Center — Required Documents by Bundle Section
text: Case Center organizes documents into Bundles (folders for specific events) and Sections (designated areas within bundles). The Pleadings section must include: Form 8 (Application), Form 10 (Answer), Form 15 (Motion to Change), Form 15B (Response to Motion to Change), Form 13 (Financial Statements), Form 35.1/35.1A (Parenting affidavits), and Affidavits of Service. The Endorsements, Orders and Judgments section must include: all previous orders and endorsements still in effect, relevant endorsements issued before Case Center use, and draft orders for judicial amendment. Do NOT upload: documents subject to publication bans or sealing orders (email to court office instead), supporting evidence for lawyer removal motions (email separately), or documents not filed with the court unless ordered by a judge.

source: Ontario Court of Justice — Case Center Guide
heading: Case Center — How to Register and Grant Access
text: Use Google Chrome or MS Edge to access Case Center (Internet Explorer is not supported). Register at https://ontariocourts.casecenter.thomsonreuters.com/ by selecting 'Register' (top right). Lawyers can invite clients via the 'People' tab → 'Add people' → 'Invite New Participant'. To grant non-lawyer access, ask the judge to grant the access. Warning: if you invite people who are not lawyers and they share confidential information about the case, the judge may order consequences, including costs. For virtual hearings, click 'View Hearings' for upcoming hearings and Zoom coordinates; lawyers can share Zoom links via the 'Video Conference Link' button. Support: 24/7 resources at https://www.thomsonreuters.ca/en/caselines/ontario-courts-support.html; self-represented litigant hotline: 1-800-980-4962 or info.CaseCenter@ontario.ca (option 4).
